title: transfer — field classification
description: Every column across xfer_jobs, xfer_shards, xfer_artifacts, and xfer_mapping_profiles classified per foundation's tier taxonomy. Note that the artifact FILE CONTENTS on disk carry the source entity's classification — an athletes-import errors.csv is regulated_minor because Athlete rows are regulated_minor — not the classification of the XferArtifact ledger row itself.

audit_log: XferJob status transitions are dual-written to activity_log (product feed) and audits (compliance evidence) via HasActivityLog and HasAudit traits on the model.
XferArtifact download requests are audited: creating a signed URL writes an audit row referencing the causer + xfer_job + artifact kind.

notes: `XferJob.source_filename` may be PII when the file name itself contains a person's name (e.g. `Jane_Doe_Roster_Q3.csv`). Confidential.
`XferJob.params` snapshots the request DTO; it may contain filter values that are PII (e.g. `filter[email]=user@example.com`). Confidential.
`XferJob.filters` is a spatie/laravel-query-builder grammar snapshot; same concern as params. Confidential.
`XferJob.last_error_message` is emitted from validation failures that MAY quote row values (e.g. 'row 42: email `alice@example.com` is invalid'). Confidential.
`XferArtifact.path` reveals internal storage structure; not credential-adjacent but not for workspace eyes either. Confidential + hidden on the wire (see schema `x-wire.hidden`).
`XferMappingProfile.header_map` reveals the column headers of the source system — may hint at PII column names (e.g. `SSN`, `child_dob`). Confidential.
`XferMappingProfile.description` is free-text user-authored; treat as confidential.

`XferJob.source_file_hash` and `XferArtifact.checksum_sha256` are restricted per foundation taxonomy — they are integrity-verification metadata and revealing them helps an attacker craft tampered replacements. Never emit on the wire; hidden in the model.

ARTIFACT FILE CONTENTS ON DISK inherit the source entity's classification. An athletes import errors.csv contains regulated_minor rows; an audit-log export contains confidential rows. Access control on downloads uses the entity permission + xfer_job policy, not this table.
